name: node-strip-eof
title: node-strip-eof
summary: strip CR and LF characters from a string/buffer
description:
strip CR and LF characters from a string/buffer
Strips CR and LF characters (i.e., it strips them away, if the string ends
with LR, CR, or CRLR, but not more if there's more of those characters).
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-strip-eof because strip CR and LF characters from a string/buffer.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-strip-eof as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-strip-eof with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-strip-eof --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
